methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Hyfix is a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by Mike Horton and Udan Ercan. The company has raised $15M to date. The relevant comp universe consists of 10 peers—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems—drawn from a broader 19-company Defense & Security sector pool.

Given the lack of sufficiently granular same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables (0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage peers identified), the Peer-Median approach was inapplicable. Instead, the Stage-Progression method anchors on Hyfix's $15M total raised as a proxy for post-Seed valuation, applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage step-ups, yielding $38M.

This analysis supports a valuation range of $32M to $43M, with a median of $38M. The range reflects ±15% variance around the stage-progression result to account for company-specific risk factors not captured by the single applicable method. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct same-stage comps in Defense & Security and limited visibility into the public comp multiples' applicability to an early-stage private company. Any valuation within this band should be stress-tested against Hyfix's specific product maturity, customer traction, and relative positioning within the frontier defense ecosystem.
